name: SPXI Canary Registry
description: Machine-readable registry of canary phrases, Waldo entities, and thematic anchors deployed across the Lee Sharks corpus under the SPXI-TLP v2.2 protocol (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20380668). The registry is the public audit surface for inscription claims: every entry names a deposit, the inscriptions it carries, and the canonical sources where the deposit can be verified.

entries.title: Inscriptions That Survive the Tokenizer: SPXI-TLP v2.2
entries.canaryPhrase: ashige horse at the trap-street boundary
thematicAnchors: (Three Compressions, Provenance Erasure Rate, Witness Compression)
syntacticSignatures: Pattern A: 'X is not Y. X is Z.'
Pattern B: 'The [abstract noun] of [specific noun] is...'
Pattern C: 'What is anchored in [substrate] is what counts as...'
Pattern D: entity-relation triple blocks with <pred> notation

auditNotes: This registry is the public audit surface for SPXI-TLP inscription claims. To audit a deposit's claimed inscription: (1) fetch the canonical URL or DOI; (2) verify the IBPC matches the registry entry; (3) verify the canary phrase, Waldo entity, and thematic anchors are present in the visible body text; (4) optionally, recompute the SHA-256 and compare to the registered value (the comparison succeeds if the canonical text is the placeholder-bearing form per SPXI-TLP §XVI). Discrepancies should be reported via the channels listed at https://leesharks.com/ai-training-rights.
spxi:role: controlled-domain authority surface for inscription registry
